documentation.text: @dev The three roles in the SSI identity lifecycle.

@dev Represents a registered stakeholder in the system.
      did:          The DID string of this stakeholder.
      role:         Their assigned role (HOLDER, ISSUER, or VERIFIER).
      authorizedBy: The admin address that granted ISSUER role (zero for others).
      registeredAt: Block timestamp of role registration.
      active:       Whether this stakeholder is currently active.

@dev Represents a Holder's consent grant to a specific Verifier.
      holderDID:    The DID of the consenting Holder.
      verifierDID:  The DID of the Verifier being granted access.
      credentialId: The specific credential the Verifier may access.
      expiresAt:    Timestamp after which the consent is no longer valid (0 = no expiry).
      granted:      Whether the consent is currently active.
@dev Reference to the DIDRegistry contract for DID resolution and validation.
@dev Reference to the CredentialStatus contract for revocation checks.
@dev The deployer address is the initial super-admin of this access control system.
@dev Maps Ethereum address => Stakeholder record.
@dev Maps an admin address to whether they have admin privileges.

@dev Consent registry. Key is keccak256(holderDID, verifierDID, credentialId).
      Allows holders to grant/revoke per-credential access to specific verifiers.
@dev Internal helper to normalize a DID string as mapping key.
@notice Emitted when a new stakeholder is registered with a role.
@notice Emitted when an admin authorizes a new Issuer.
@notice Emitted when a Holder grants consent to a Verifier for a specific credential.
@notice Emitted when a Holder revokes a previously granted consent.

@param _didRegistry        Address of the deployed DIDRegistry contract.
 @param _credentialStatus   Address of the deployed CredentialStatus contract.

documentation.text:
@dev On-chain anchor record for an issued Verifiable Credential.
      issuer:          The Ethereum address (DID controller) that issued the credential.
      subjectDID:      The DID of the credential subject (holder).
      credentialHash:  Keccak256 hash of the off-chain VC JSON payload (integrity anchor).
      ipfsCID:         The IPFS Content Identifier where the encrypted VC is stored.
      issuedAt:        Block timestamp when the credential anchor was written on-chain.
      statusIndex:     The index of this credential in the issuer's revocation bitmap.
@dev Maps a credential ID (bytes32 hash of issuer+subject+nonce) to its on-chain anchor.
@dev Tracks which credential IDs have been registered.

@dev Bitmap-based revocation list.
      revocationBitmaps[issuer][bitmapChunk] is a uint256 where each bit
      represents the revocation status of one credential (1 = revoked, 0 = valid).
      This allows 256 credentials per storage slot, minimizing gas costs.
@dev Tracks the next available status index per issuer for bitmap allocation.
@notice Emitted when a new credential anchor is written on-chain by an issuer.
@notice Emitted when an issuer revokes a previously issued credential.
@notice Emitted when a verifier triggers an on-chain verification event (for auditability).

typeDescriptions.typeString: literal_string "CredentialStatus: Caller is not the issuer"

argumentTypes.typeString: literal_string "CredentialStatus: Caller is not the issuer"

documentation.text: @dev Restricts revocation to the original issuer of a credential.

@dev Represents a public key associated with a DID.
      keyId:       A short identifier for this key (e.g., "key-1").
      keyType:     The cryptographic key type (e.g., "EcdsaSecp256k1VerificationKey2019").
      controller:  The DID that controls this key.
      publicKeyHex: The hex-encoded public key material.

@dev Represents a service endpoint in the DID document.
      serviceId:   A short identifier for this service (e.g., "LinkedDomains").
      serviceType: The type of service (e.g., "VerifiableCredentialService").
      endpoint:    The URL or URI of the service.

@dev The on-chain DID Document. Stores only metadata anchors, never raw PII.
      controller:        The Ethereum address that owns and controls this DID.
      created:           Block timestamp of DID registration.
      updated:           Block timestamp of the last update.
      active:            Whether the DID is currently active (not deactivated).
      publicKeys:        Array of cryptographic keys associated with this DID.
      serviceEndpoints:  Array of service endpoints declared in the DID document.
@dev Maps a DID string (e.g., "did:ethr:0x...") to its on-chain DID document.
@dev Tracks which DIDs have been registered to prevent duplicates.
@dev Maps an Ethereum address to the DIDs it controls (for lookup convenience).
@notice Emitted when a new DID is registered on-chain.
@notice Emitted when a DID document is updated (key or service change).
@notice Emitted when a DID is deactivated by its controller.

15 - CSE 540 (Revat Saharan, Abhilakshay Sreehari, Satwik Mazumdar, Sreehari Krishna Sadesh, Shashank Valayaputtur)","details":"Design Rationale:      - Role assignments are anchored to DIDs (not raw Ethereum addresses) to ensure        that identity context is preserved even if a user rotates their keys.      - The contract integrates with DIDRegistry to validate that a DID is active        before granting or checking permissions.      - Admins (trusted entities, e.g., universities, hospitals) can authorize Issuers.      - Verifiers are self-registered but subject to Holder consent for data access.","kind":"dev","methods":{"authorizeIssuer(address,string)":{"details":"Issuers are trusted entities (e.g., universities, hospitals) granted by admins.","params":{"did":"The Issuer's active DID.","issuerAddress":"The Ethereum address of the entity to authorize as Issuer."}},"constructor":{"params":{"_credentialStatus":"Address of the deployed CredentialStatus contract.","_didRegistry":"Address of the deployed DIDRegistry contract."}},"getRole(address)":{"params":{"account":"The address to query."},"returns":{"_0":"The Role enum value (NONE, HOLDER, ISSUER, or VERIFIER)."}},"getStakeholder(address)":{"params":{"account":"The Ethereum address to query."},"returns":{"_0":"The Stakeholder struct with DID, role, and registration details."}},"grantConsent(string,bytes32,uint256)":{"details":"Called by a Holder to authorize a Verifier to view a credential.         Consent is scoped to a single credentialId to enforce data minimization.","params":{"credentialId":"The specific credential being consented to.","expiresAt":"Unix timestamp for consent expiry (0 for no expiry).","verifierDID":"The DID of the Verifier being granted access."}},"hasConsent(string,string,bytes32)":{"params":{"credentialId":"The credential being accessed.","holderDID":"The DID of the Holder who issued the consent.","verifierDID":"The DID of the Verifier requesting access."},"returns":{"_0":"True if consent is granted and not expired."}},"registerAsHolder(string)":{"details":"Requires that the caller has an active DID in the DIDRegistry.         Holders self-register; no admin approval is needed.","params":{"did":"The caller's DID string (must be active in DIDRegistry)."}},"registerAsVerifier(string)":{"details":"Verifiers self-register but require an active DID.         They can only access credentials with explicit Holder consent.","params":{"did":"The caller's DID string (must be active in DIDRegistry)."}},"revokeConsent(string,bytes32)":{"details":"Called by a Holder to withdraw a Verifier's access to a credential.","params":{"credentialId":"The credential for which consent is revoked.","verifierDID":"The DID of the Verifier whose access is being revoked."}}},"stateVariables":{"admins":{"details":"Maps an admin address to whether they have admin privileges."},"consentGrants":{"details":"Consent registry. Key is keccak256(holderDID, verifierDID, credentialId).      Allows holders to grant/revoke per-credential access to specific verifiers."},"credentialStatus":{"details":"Reference to the CredentialStatus contract for revocation checks."},"didRegistry":{"details":"Reference to the DIDRegistry contract for DID resolution and validation."},"stakeholders":{"details":"Maps Ethereum address => Stakeholder record."},"superAdmin":{"details":"The deployer address is the initial super-admin of this access control system."}},"title":"AccessControl","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"ConsentGranted(string,string,bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a Holder grants consent to a Verifier for a specific credential."},"ConsentRevoked(string,string,bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a Holder revokes a previously granted consent."},"IssuerAuthorized(address,string,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when an admin authorizes a new Issuer."},"StakeholderRegistered(address,string,uint8,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a new stakeholder is registered with a role."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"authorizeIssuer(address,string)":{"notice":"Authorizes a specific address as an Issuer. Admin-only."},"getRole(address)":{"notice":"Returns the role of a given Ethereum address."},"getStakeholder(address)":{"notice":"Returns the full Stakeholder record for a given address."},"grantConsent(string,bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Grants a specific Verifier access to a specific credential."},"hasConsent(string,string,bytes32)":{"notice":"Checks whether a Verifier has valid consent to access a specific credential."},"registerAsHolder(string)":{"notice":"Registers the caller as a Holder in the system."},"registerAsVerifier(string)":{"notice":"Registers the caller as a Verifier in the system."},"revokeConsent(string,bytes32)":{"notice":"Revokes a previously granted consent."}},"notice":"Governs role-based access management within the IAM dApp.         Defines and enforces the three core stakeholder roles:         Issuer, Verifier, and Holder (User/Subject).","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/AccessControl.sol":"AccessControl"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/AccessControl.sol":{"keccak256":"0x32bcb598e54cec8531330e10c13eea601c2e35dddb8a137c6871c58e9790ba57","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://b25dd479ac0d184d5c6e1ea57b0e0f6fbde37a0acb0d65d596d2817ba177dfd9","dweb:/ipfs/QmWMNtEmU9somHgcKrompKcJZ67ssuqwdZkwLrZVZJW95j"]},"contracts/CredentialStatus.sol":{"keccak256":"0x2499c842a33583d569a3f0f2f598e0907577116f95f3ced55b82d3ff02f33b28","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://f62a9bee5bf450cba9c23153e44a4e4653a386b524d64b9b3db8729b3cd1f67e","dweb:/ipfs/QmQGGH1KZP7pqT4nbqx1u414KneKuPoQV1CChHEzNaGJHb"]},"contracts/DIDRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0xe3d33c9ef06811d6b0834e65f1f6fdf90c62beb586133769058ea001f0ddaf9f","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://815b6f7d6d9699f3341b5c65a48bdebc67a87b44f334e0af61b6c684786b9c79","dweb:/ipfs/QmYEFhF8A1n77GEV68Y8e6vzFrfwA2CDo34tm92ibi6P6s"]}},"version":1}

{"compiler":{"version":"0.8.20+commit.a1b79de6"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"},{"indexed":true,"internalType":"address","name":"issuer","type":"address"},{"indexed":true,"internalType":"string","name":"subjectDID","type":"string"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialHash","type":"bytes32"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"string","name":"ipfsCID","type":"string"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"statusIndex","type":"uint256"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"timestamp","type":"uint256"}],"name":"CredentialIssued","type":"event"},{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"},{"indexed":true,"internalType":"address","name":"issuer","type":"address"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"statusIndex","type":"uint256"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"timestamp","type":"uint256"}],"name":"CredentialRevoked","type":"event"},{"anonymous":false,"inputs":[{"indexed":true,"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"},{"indexed":true,"internalType":"address","name":"verifier","type":"address"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"bool","name":"result","type":"bool"},{"indexed":false,"internalType":"uint256","name":"timestamp","type":"uint256"}],"name":"VerificationLogged","type":"event"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"getCredentialAnchor","outputs":[{"components":[{"internalType":"address","name":"issuer","type":"address"},{"internalType":"string","name":"subjectDID","type":"string"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialHash","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"string","name":"ipfsCID","type":"string"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"issuedAt","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"statusIndex","type":"uint256"}],"internalType":"struct CredentialStatus.CredentialAnchor","name":"","type":"tuple"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"isRevoked","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"string","name":"subjectDID","type":"string"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialHash","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"string","name":"ipfsCID","type":"string"}],"name":"issueCredential","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"result","type":"bool"}],"name":"logVerification","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"revokeCredential","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"credentialId","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"computedHash","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"verifyCredentialIntegrity","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"author":"Group 15 - CSE 540 (Revat Saharan, Abhilakshay Sreehari, Satwik Mazumdar, Sreehari Krishna Sadesh, Shashank Valayaputtur)","details":"Design Rationale:      - Storing full VCs on-chain is gas-prohibitive and risks PII exposure.      - Instead, only a cryptographic hash (commitment) of each VC is anchored here.      - Verifiers fetch the full VC from IPFS, recompute the hash, and compare it to        the on-chain commitment to verify data integrity.      - The bitmap revocation list allows O(1) status checks with minimal gas overhead.      - Complies with the W3C StatusList2021 specification concept.","kind":"dev","methods":{"getCredentialAnchor(bytes32)":{"params":{"credentialId":"The unique identifier of the credential to retrieve."},"returns":{"_0":"The CredentialAnchor struct with IPFS CID, hash, and metadata."}},"isRevoked(bytes32)":{"details":"Reads the bitmap bit at the credential's statusIndex.","params":{"credentialId":"The unique identifier of the credential to check."},"returns":{"_0":"True if the credential has been revoked, false if still valid."}},"issueCredential(string,bytes32,string)":{"details":"Called by the Issuer after signing the VC off-chain with EIP-712.         Only the IPFS CID and credential hash are stored \u2014 no raw credential data.         Automatically allocates a status bitmap index for future revocation.","params":{"credentialHash":"Keccak256 hash of the full VC JSON payload.","ipfsCID":"The IPFS CID pointing to the encrypted VC off-chain.","subjectDID":"The DID of the credential subject (holder)."},"returns":{"credentialId":"  A unique bytes32 identifier for this credential anchor."}},"logVerification(bytes32,bool)":{"details":"Does not enforce access; purely an event-emission for transparency.         Verifiers call this to leave an immutable audit trail without PII.","params":{"credentialId":"The credential being verified.","result":"The result of the off-chain cryptographic verification."}},"revokeCredential(bytes32)":{"details":"Only the original issuer may revoke. Sets the bit at statusIndex to 1.         This is a gas-efficient O(1) operation using bitwise OR.","params":{"credentialId":"The unique identifier of the credential anchor to revoke."}},"verifyCredentialIntegrity(bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Verifiers fetch the encrypted VC from IPFS, decrypt it, then call this to confirm         the data has not been tampered with.","params":{"computedHash":"The keccak256 hash of the locally fetched and decrypted VC payload.","credentialId":"The credential anchor ID to verify against."},"returns":{"_0":"True if the hash matches the on-chain commitment (data integrity confirmed)."}}},"stateVariables":{"credentialAnchors":{"details":"Maps a credential ID (bytes32 hash of issuer+subject+nonce) to its on-chain anchor."},"nextStatusIndex":{"details":"Tracks the next available status index per issuer for bitmap allocation."},"registeredCredentials":{"details":"Tracks which credential IDs have been registered."},"revocationBitmaps":{"details":"Bitmap-based revocation list.      revocationBitmaps[issuer][bitmapChunk] is a uint256 where each bit      represents the revocation status of one credential (1 = revoked, 0 = valid).      This allows 256 credentials per storage slot, minimizing gas costs."}},"title":"CredentialStatus","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"CredentialIssued(bytes32,address,string,bytes32,string,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a new credential anchor is written on-chain by an issuer."},"CredentialRevoked(bytes32,address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when an issuer revokes a previously issued credential."},"VerificationLogged(bytes32,address,bool,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a verifier triggers an on-chain verification event (for auditability)."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"getCredentialAnchor(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns the on-chain anchor data for a given credential ID."},"isRevoked(bytes32)":{"notice":"Checks whether a credential has been revoked."},"issueCredential(string,bytes32,string)":{"notice":"Anchors a new Verifiable Credential on-chain after off-chain signing."},"logVerification(bytes32,bool)":{"notice":"Logs a verification attempt on-chain for auditability."},"revokeCredential(bytes32)":{"notice":"Revokes an existing credential by flipping its bit in the revocation bitmap."},"verifyCredentialIntegrity(bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Verifies the integrity of a fetched VC by comparing its hash to the on-chain commitment."}},"notice":"Manages the on-chain revocation and status registry for Verifiable Credentials (VCs).         Uses a gas-efficient bitmap approach: each credential is assigned an index in a         bitmap, and its revocation status is stored as a single bit flip.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/CredentialStatus.sol":"CredentialStatus"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/CredentialStatus.sol":{"keccak256":"0x2499c842a33583d569a3f0f2f598e0907577116f95f3ced55b82d3ff02f33b28","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://f62a9bee5bf450cba9c23153e44a4e4653a386b524d64b9b3db8729b3cd1f67e","dweb:/ipfs/QmQGGH1KZP7pqT4nbqx1u414KneKuPoQV1CChHEzNaGJHb"]}},"version":1}
